title: APUSH LEQ thesis and CC practice

terms.term: Evaluate the extent to which the market revolution transformed labor systems in the period 1800-1850.
terms.definition: thesis: The market revolution fundamentally transformed labor by shifting workers from household production to wage labor in factories and commercial agriculture. cc: Before the market revolution, most Americans worked in household-based subsistence farming, but after transportation improvements and factory growth, wage labor and cash-crop agriculture expanded dramatically.
CONTEXT: In the early nineteenth century, improved transportation and industrialization reshaped how Americans produced goods.
CLAIM: The market revolution transformed labor systems by replacing household self-sufficiency with wage labor and commercial farming.
EVIDENCE: Lowell textile mills employed young women for cash wages, and Midwestern farmers shifted to wheat and corn for distant markets after the Erie Canal opened.
